headline: Higher gas prices could spell recession risks to some US consumers
summary: Americans are growing increasingly worried about the surge in US gas prices (RB=F), as signs at the pump are now approaching a national average of $4.00 per gallon.Citi Director of retailing/broadlines & hardlines Steven Zaccone comes on Market Catalysts to discuss how gas price pressures are hitting both all-tiers of US consumers.

headline: Costco’s Amazing Success
summary: Costco’s (NASDAQ: COST) shares have defied the market downturn driven by inflation worries, the war in the Middle East, and a decline in consumer confidence. Its stock is up 14% this year while the S&P 500 has dropped 7%. Its costs should rise soon, however, because the prices of hundreds of items will be affected ... Costco’s Amazing Success

headline: Wall Street Upgrades Celsius Holdings: Costco Fear Overdone and the Franchise Is Still Growing
summary: Deutsche Bank analyst Steve Powers upgraded Celsius Holdings (NASDAQ:CELH) to Buy from Hold on Monday, setting a price target of $44, down from $56. The firm sees a buying opportunity after the stock’s sharp recent decline, arguing that the market has overreacted to concerns about Costco (NASDAQ:COST) launching a competing private-label energy drink. Ticker Firm Rating ... Wall Street Upgrades Celsius Holdings: Costco Fear Overdone and the Franchise Is Still Growing

headline: Target Is Up 44%. Has It Finally Turned the Corner on Its Troubles?
summary: Retailers entered 2026 still sorting through uneven consumer budgets. Inflation has cooled, yet discretionary spending remains picky — food holds steady while big-ticket items lag. Some chains expanded; others posted their third straight year of flat or falling sales. Against that backdrop, Target (NYSE:TGT) shares have climbed 44% from the 52-week low of $83.44 reached ... Target Is Up 44%. Has It Finally Turned the Corner on Its Troubles?
